regime_fall.current_status: The 60-day US-Iran conflict has escalated beyond ceasefire with Trump's shoot-on-sight orders for Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, direct tanker seizures causing 10%+ GDP loss, and IRGC retaliatory vessel seizures. Tehran's leadership fractures under simultaneous military pressure, economic collapse, internal unrest requiring foreign militia deployment, and succession uncertainty as Ghalibaf resigns and Araghchi pursues diplomatic off-ramps in Islamabad while hardliners like Vahidi block negotiations.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 24, 2026, Trump ordered US forces to 'shoot and kill' Iranian vessels laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz and enforced naval blockade with direct tanker seizures, while the Treasury froze $344M in Iranian cryptocurrency and sanctioned Chinese refineries, marking full economic-military escalation after ceasefire expiration on April 22.
